tex:
Lena owns a café.
\newline

(a) One day, Lena records the drinks she sells in one hour.
\newline

    \begin{tabular}{cccccccc}
    Tea & Tea & Coffee & Juice & Milkshake & Milkshake & Coffee & Coffee \\
    Milkshake & Coffee & Tea & Juice & Tea & Coffee & Tea & Juice \\
    Milkshake & Tea & Milkshake & Tea & Coffee & Tea & Milkshake & Coffee
    \end{tabular}
    (i)  Complete the frequency table.
\newline

        You may use the tally column to help you.
\newline

        \begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
        \hline
        Drink & Tally & Frequency \\
        \hline
        Coffee & & \\
        \hline
        Juice & & \\
        \hline
        Milkshake & & \\
        \hline
        Tea & & \\
        \hline
        & Total & 24 \\
        \hline
        \end{tabular}

         Draw a bar chart to show this information.
\newline

        Complete the scale on the frequency axis.
\newline

(b) This table shows the opening hours of the café.
\newline

    \begin{tabular}{|l|c|}
    \hline
    Day & Opening hours \\
    \hline
    Monday to Thursday & 08$\,$30 to 17$\,$00 \\
    \hline
    Friday and Saturday & 08$\,$30 to 19$\,$00 \\
    \hline
    Sunday & 09$\,$00 to 18$\,$00 \\
    \hline
    \end{tabular}
    (i)  Work out the total number of hours the café is open in one week.
\newline

        

         Lena is in the café for 40 hours each week.
\newline

        Ron is in the café when Lena is not there.
\newline

        Calculate the percentage of the total opening hours that Ron is in the café each week.
\newline

        

(c) Saddak buys 3 cups of tea and 2 cookies for \$6.95.
\newline

    A cup of tea costs \$1.75.
\newline

    Work out the cost of one cookie.
\newline

    

(d) The price of a cake is \$2.60.
\newline

    At the end of the day, Lena reduces the price of each cake by 35\%.
\newline

    Calculate the reduced price of a cake.
\newline

content.content:
One day, Lena records the drinks she sells in one hour.
\newline

Complete the frequency table.
\newline

        You may use the tally column to help you.
\newline

\begin{center}
        \begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
        \hline
        Drink & Tally & Frequency \\
        \hline
        Coffee & & \\
        \hline
        Juice & & \\
        \hline
        Milkshake & & \\
        \hline
        Tea & & \\
        \hline
        & Total & 24 \\
        \hline
        \end{tabular}
        \end{center}

Draw a bar chart to show this information.
\newline

        Complete the scale on the frequency axis.
\newline

This table shows the opening hours of the café.
\newline

Work out the total number of hours the café is open in one week.
\newline

Lena is in the café for 40 hours each week.
\newline

        Ron is in the café when Lena is not there.
\newline

        Calculate the percentage of the total opening hours that Ron is in the café each week.
\newline

Saddak buys 3 cups of tea and 2 cookies for \$6.95.
\newline

    A cup of tea costs \$1.75.
\newline

    Work out the cost of one cookie.
\newline

The price of a cake is \$2.60.
\newline

    At the end of the day, Lena reduces the price of each cake by 35\%.
\newline

    Calculate the reduced price of a cake.
\newline

qtype: various number operations: factors, primes, calculation with decimals, powers, roots, indices, prime factorization
tex:
(a) Write down all the factors of 18.
\newline

    

(b) Write down a prime number between 40 and 50.
\newline

    

(c) Calculate $1.09 + \dfrac{7.85}{6.21 - 4.37}$.
\newline

    Give your answer correct to 1 decimal place.
\newline

    

(d) Find the value of

    (i)  $\sqrt{2.89}$,

        

         $14^3$,

        

         $4^{-2}$.
\newline

        

(e) (i)  $126 = 2 \times 3^2 \times k$

        Find the value of $k$.
\newline

        

         Write 90 as the product of its prime factors.
\newline

        

         Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of 90 and 126.
\newline

content.content:
Write down all the factors of 18.
\newline

Write down a prime number between 40 and 50.
\newline

Calculate $1.09 + \dfrac{7.85}{6.21 - 4.37}$.
\newline

    Give your answer correct to 1 decimal place.
\newline
Find the value of

$4^{-2}$.
\newline

$126 = 2 \times 3^2 \times k$

        Find the value of $k$.
\newline

Write 90 as the product of its prime factors.
\newline

Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of 90 and 126.
\newline

qtype: arithmetic operations, fractions, ratios from tabular data
tex:
(a) The table gives some information about the numbers of visitors at a leisure centre one day.
\newline

    \begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|}
    \hline
    & Adult & Child & Total \\
    \hline
    Male & & 144 & 240 \\
    \hline
    Female & 129 & & 260 \\
    \hline
    Total & 225 & 275 & 500 \\
    \hline
    \end{tabular}
    (i)  Complete the table.
\newline

         Work out how many more child visitors than adult visitors there are.
\newline

        

         Write down the fraction of visitors that are adults.
\newline

        Give your answer in its lowest terms.
\newline

        

         Write the ratio   number of males : number of females.
\newline

        Give your answer in its simplest form.
\newline

        

         One of these visitors is selected at random.
\newline

        Find the probability that this visitor is a male child.
\newline

        

(b) The number of people in each of 150 cars entering the leisure centre car park is recorded.
\newline

    The table shows the results.
\newline

    \begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|}
    \hline
    Number of people & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 \\
    \hline
    Frequency & 44 & 43 & 30 & 25 & 8 \\
    \hline
    \end{tabular}
    (i)  Write down the mode.
\newline

        

         Calculate the mean.
\newline

        

(c) In a survey of 50 visitors to the leisure centre, 18 used the gym.
\newline

    One day, 1500 people visited the leisure centre.
\newline

    Calculate an estimate for the number of people who used the gym on this day.
\newline

content.content:
The table gives some information about the numbers of visitors at a leisure centre one day.
\newline

Complete the table.
\newline

Work out how many more child visitors than adult visitors there are.
\newline

Write down the fraction of visitors that are adults.
\newline

        Give your answer in its lowest terms.
\newline

Write the ratio \quad number of males : number of females.
\newline

        Give your answer in its simplest form.
\newline

One of these visitors is selected at random.
\newline

        Find the probability that this visitor is a male child.
\newline

The number of people in each of 150 cars entering the leisure centre car park is recorded.
\newline

    The table shows the results.
\newline

Write down the mode.
\newline

Calculate the mean.
\newline

In a survey of 50 visitors to the leisure centre, 18 used the gym.
\newline

    One day, 1500 people visited the leisure centre.
\newline

    Calculate an estimate for the number of people who used the gym on this day.
\newline

qtype: drawing quadratic graph and solving equation graphically
tex:
(a) (i)  Complete the table of values for $y = x^2 - 5x$.
\newline

        \begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
        \hline
        $x$ & $-1$ & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 6 \\
        \hline
        $y$ & & & $-4$ & $-6$ & $-6$ & $-4$ & 0 & \\
        \hline
        \end{tabular}

         On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^2 - 5x$ for $-1 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$.
\newline

         Write down the co-ordinates of the lowest point of your graph.
\newline

        

         Use your graph to solve the equation $x^2 - 5x = 3$.
\newline

        

(b) 
    Line $L$ is drawn on the grid.
\newline

    (i)  Find the equation of line $L$ in the form $y = mx + c$.
\newline

        

         Line $P$ is parallel to line $L$ and passes through the point $(0, -1)$.
\newline

        On the grid above, draw line $P$ for $-5 \leqslant x \leqslant 5$.
\newline

content.content:
Complete the table of values for $y = x^2 - 5x$.
\newline

On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^2 - 5x$ for $-1 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$.
\newline

Write down the co-ordinates of the lowest point of your graph.
\newline

Use your graph to solve the equation $x^2 - 5x = 3$.
\newline

Find the equation of line $L$ in the form $y = mx + c$.
\newline

Line $P$ is parallel to line $L$ and passes through the point $(0, -1)$.
\newline

        On the grid above, draw line $P$ for $-5 \leqslant x \leqslant 5$.
\newline

tex:
(a) The scale drawing shows the positions of a lighthouse $L$ and a ship $S$.
\newline

    The scale is 1 centimetre represents 5 kilometres.
\newline

    (i)  Work out the actual distance, in kilometres, from $S$ to $L$.
\newline

        

         Measure the bearing of $S$ from $L$.
\newline

        

         Another ship, $T$, is $22\,\text{km}$ from $L$ on a bearing of $210°$.
\newline

        Mark and label the position of $T$ on the scale drawing.
\newline

(b) **In this part, use a ruler and compasses only and show your construction arcs clearly.
\newline
**

    The scale drawing shows the positions of two yachts, $P$ and $Q$.
\newline

    The scale is 1 centimetre represents 100 metres.
\newline

    (i)  Construct the locus of points equidistant from $P$ and $Q$.
\newline

         Another yacht, $Y$, is
        (c) closer to $P$ than to $Q$
        and
        (d) less than $700\,\text{m}$ from $Q$.
\newline
        On the scale drawing, construct and shade the region where yacht $Y$ is.
\newline

content.content:
Work out the actual distance, in kilometres, from $S$ to $L$.
\newline

Measure the bearing of $S$ from $L$.
\newline

Another ship, $T$, is $22\,\text{km}$ from $L$ on a bearing of $210°$.
\newline

        Mark and label the position of $T$ on the scale drawing.
\newline

Construct the locus of points equidistant from $P$ and $Q$.
\newline

Another yacht, $Y$, is
        \begin{itemize}

less than $700\,\text{m}$ from $Q$.
\newline
        \end{itemize}

        On the scale drawing, construct and shade the region where yacht $Y$ is.
\newline

qtype: fraction of a quantity and remaining mass
tex:
Nora makes a birthday cake.
\newline

(a) Nora has a packet containing $250\,\text{g}$ of cherries.
\newline

    She uses $\dfrac{7}{10}$ of the cherries in the cake.
\newline

    Find the mass of cherries she has left.
\newline

    

(b) The cake is made by putting a small cylinder of cake on top of a large cylinder of cake.
\newline

    The radius of the large cylinder is $15\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

    The radius of the small cylinder is $8\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

    The height of each cylinder is $10\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

    (i)  Calculate the total volume of the cake.
\newline

        

         Nora wraps a ribbon around the large cylinder.
\newline

        The ribbon is $4\,\text{cm}$ longer than needed to go all the way around this cylinder.
\newline

        Calculate the length of this ribbon.
\newline

        

(c) The mass, $m$ grams, of the cake is $1250\,\text{g}$, correct to the nearest $10\,\text{g}$.
\newline

    Complete this statement about the value of $m$.
\newline

content.content:
Nora has a packet containing $250\,\text{g}$ of cherries.
\newline

    She uses $\dfrac{7}{10}$ of the cherries in the cake.
\newline

    Find the mass of cherries she has left.
\newline

Calculate the total volume of the cake.
\newline

Nora wraps a ribbon around the large cylinder.
\newline

        The ribbon is $4\,\text{cm}$ longer than needed to go all the way around this cylinder.
\newline

        Calculate the length of this ribbon.
\newline

The mass, $m$ grams, of the cake is $1250\,\text{g}$, correct to the nearest $10\,\text{g}$.
\newline

    Complete this statement about the value of $m$.
\newline

qtype: simplifying expressions, substitution, solving linear equations, rearranging formula
tex:
(a) Simplify.
\newline
    \[4c + 2d - c + 6d\]

    

(b) $h = 5m - 2n$

    Calculate $h$ when $m = 4$ and $n = -6$.
\newline

    

(c) Solve.
\newline
    \[7(x - 3) = 56\]

    

(d) Make $t$ the subject of the formula $r = 6t + 7$.
\newline

    

(e) The diagram shows a triangle.
\newline

    Use the diagram to write down an equation and solve it to find the value of $x$.
\newline

content.content:
Simplify.
\newline
    \[4c + 2d - c + 6d\]

$h = 5m - 2n$

    Calculate $h$ when $m = 4$ and $n = -6$.
\newline

Solve.
\newline
    \[7(x - 3) = 56\]

Make $t$ the subject of the formula $r = 6t + 7$.
\newline

tex:
The diagram shows a field in the shape of a trapezium.
\newline

$AB = 150\,\text{m}$, $BC = 90\,\text{m}$ and $CD = 120\,\text{m}$.
\newline

Angle $ABC$ = angle $BCD$ = $90°$.
\newline

(a) Calculate the area of the field.
\newline

    

(b) (i)  Show that $AD = 95\,\text{m}$, correct to the nearest metre.
\newline

         A fence is built around the perimeter of the field.
\newline

        It costs \$48 to build each 5-metre section of the fence.
\newline

        Calculate the cost of building this fence.
\newline

content.content:
Calculate the area of the field.
\newline

Show that $AD = 95\,\text{m}$, correct to the nearest metre.
\newline

A fence is built around the perimeter of the field.
\newline

        It costs \$48 to build each 5-metre section of the fence.
\newline

        Calculate the cost of building this fence.
\newline
